tasks.title: mixed-signal electrochemical biosensor front end driven by one-compartment oral pharmacokinetic concentration profile
tasks.prompt: Build a Simscape teaching example for a pharmacokinetic drug concentration input driving an electrochemical biosensor front end. Use a one-compartment oral PK profile C(t) as the input signal, convert concentration to sensor current with sensitivity 25 nA/uM and first-order membrane mass-transfer lag tau_m = 4 s, then a transimpedance amplifier Rf = 1 MOhm with Cf = 10 nF and a 12-bit ADC. Probe concentration, sensor current, TIA output, and settling error. Teach PK input, mass-transfer lag, TIA bandwidth/noise tradeoff, and ADC saturation.
tasks.probe_ask_text: 测量 稳定 误差

tasks.title: simplified two-electrode voltage-clamp cell membrane with passive membrane, leak reversal potential, clamp amplifier, and series electrode resistance
tasks.prompt: Build a Simscape teaching example for a two-electrode voltage-clamp cell membrane. Model the membrane as Cm = 100 pF in parallel with a leak conductance of 10 nS, with a resting reversal source near -70 mV. Drive a command step from -70 mV to -20 mV through a clamp amplifier and a 5 MOhm series electrode resistance. Probe membrane voltage, clamp current, and electrode voltage drop. Teach capacitive transient current, leak current, series-resistance clamp error, and why the probe reference node matters.

tasks.title: passive RC low-pass anti-alias front end for ECG teaching example
tasks.summary: Teaching plan ready. Probe or export evidence next.
tasks.prompt: Build a Simscape teaching example for an ECG anti-alias front end. Input is a 1 mV, 5 Hz ECG-like sensor plus 60 Hz interference. Use an RC low-pass with R = 39.8 kOhm and C = 100 nF, probe Vout relative to electrical reference, then sample with a 500 Hz ADC. Teach cutoff frequency, Nyquist edge attenuation, output-node probe placement, and what goes wrong if the capacitor is accidentally 100 uF.
